hallucinations.description: The paper repeatedly cites Rondina et al. (2025) as the source of the Data Transparency Scorecard (DTS) framework, its six-section taxonomy, and all inverse-frequency weights. It also cites Oreamuno et al. (2024) for the 71.52% undocumented HuggingFace statistic. Both are explicitly marked UNVERIFIED in the references section, with the paper stating they have not been found in Semantic Scholar or other literature databases. These appear to be fabricated citations.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: 'Rondina et al. (2025). [UNVERIFIED -- Title and venue not confirmed...]' and 'Oreamuno et al. (2024). [UNVERIFIED -- Title and venue not confirmed...]'. Introduction and Section 2.1 repeatedly rely on these for DTS weights and prior results.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found: primarily nonexistent/fake citations for the core DTS framework and prior empirical work. The methodology, code, and experimental results appear internally consistent with real API data collection, but the unverifiable citations undermine the paper's scholarly integrity. Severity is moderate-high due to reliance on these sources for all key claims.
